mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with declining housing starts signals a rotation from cyclical construction to manufacturing and technology sectors, shifting capital flows toward higher-valuation equities and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in forward 60-day returns during the specified regime, or the cross-sectional correlation between INDPRO and HOUST breaks down without affecting SPX direction.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPI exceeds 300, rising real money supply indicates liquidity injection that temporarily supports equity valuations despite rate hikes, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0 percent and RSAFS rises, or the liquidity effect disappears once inflation expectations decouple from headline CPI.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real GDP growth signals expanding aggregate demand, which directly lifts corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days without requiring specific thresholds.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 growth surprises, or the signal generates losses in more than 55% of quarters with positive GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index drops below 95.0, supply chain bottlenecks or capacity constraints trigger cost-push inflation and margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where INDPRO drops below 95.0, or the decline in industrial output fails to correlate with equity drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in payroll employment and increases in the federal funds rate signal a policy-induced labor market contraction that suppresses consumer demand and earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative equity returns when PAYEMS falls and FEDFUNDS rises, or forward returns remain positive despite confirmed labor market deterioration and rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid housing market cooling where housing starts fall more than 10 percent month-over-month, the resulting wealth effect contraction reduces consumer spending and corporate revenues, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when HOUST declines sharply alongside stable PAYEMS, or the housing wealth effect fails to transmit to broader equity valuations.
